text: The surgeon uses the fourth robotic arm with a Raytech to mobilize the liver, dividing the hepatorenal ligament. Short hepatic vein branches are sequentially divided using robotic metal clips or a vessel sealer. The triangular ligament is divided, and larger short hepatic vein branches are controlled with hemlock clips. The camera is adjusted to a thirty-degree up position for improved visualization as the triangular ligaments are divided towards the right hepatic vein.

During the retrohepatic caval dissection, the surgeon identifies and controls the inferior hepatic vein accessory branches using hemlock clips. Larger branches are divided with a robotic stapler. The liver is fully mobilized, and the base of the right hepatic vein is visualized, indicating readiness for the robotic right hepatectomy.
The surgeon dissects the extrahepatic right hepatic vein near the inferior vena cava, placing a Raytech sponge laterally to the vena cava to establish a clear plane for liver division.
